Meta.title: Code-Site.Art — Custom websites that book meetings 24/7. Live in 4–10 weeks.
Meta.description: Boutique studio in Kyiv shipping custom-coded sites for SMBs and startups in the US, EU, and DK. Fixed price from £1,000. 1-year warranty + 30% rebate if we miss the deadline.

Footer.brandDesc: Boutique custom website studio — 50+ projects over 5 years across 7 regions: UA · EU · US · DK · ZA · UK · FR.

Newsletter.heading: How not to burn your website budget
Newsletter.sub: Once a month, one real-project breakdown with numbers: what worked, what didn't, what it cost. For business owners thinking about commissioning a site. No news, no fluff.

LaunchCta.heading: Let's launch a website that works for your business
LaunchCta.sub: Book a consultation and we'll go through your project: structure, functionality, timeline, and budget. We'll explain what you need and where spending money won't pay off.
LaunchCta.imageAlt: Website mockup on a laptop and smartphone

meta.title: Website Cost Calculator — Code-Site.Art
meta.description: Estimate your custom-coded website in 60 seconds. Honest range, no 'price on request', no sales pressure. See what drives the price.

pageHero.title: Know your website price in 60 seconds
pageHero.sub: No sales call, no “price on request”, no hidden lines. An honest range, and a clear breakdown of what drives it. Built for people deciding right now.

howItWorks.title: How this estimate works
howItWorks.sub: Three layers shape every project. The calculator separates a simple site from a scalable business platform.

base.title: Base project
base.body: Website type, CMS setup, responsive layout, and launch-ready structure.

scope.title: Scope add-ons
scope.body: Pages, languages, design level, integrations, and content increase the project scope.

final.title: Final review
final.body: The calculator gives a realistic range. Final price is confirmed after scope review.

whyPackages.title: Three packages cover 80% of requests. Here's why.
whyPackages.sub: Each one solves a different business question. Pick the one that matches where you are now, not where you might be in 3 years.

starter.title: Starter
starter.body: For people launching one service or testing a hypothesis. No CMS, no 20 pages. You need to validate demand fast.

growth.title: Growth
growth.body: For people with paying clients but no website that sells. Cases, blog, SEO, lead form: everything that brings traffic and converts it into calls.

ecommerce.title: E-commerce
ecommerce.body: For people selling products. A real store: cart, payments, and a CMS to manage stock.

customizer.title: Configure your scope
customizer.sub: Start with a recommended package or build it manually below. Numbers update live.

afterLaunch.title: Optional monthly support
afterLaunch.sub: Maintenance keeps the site stable. SEO & Growth grows traffic and leads. Both are optional and separate from the project price.

maintenance.title: Maintenance / after-launch
maintenance.note: Monthly technical support — separate from the one-time project estimate.

growth.title: SEO & Growth monthly
growth.note: Ongoing SEO, content, analytics, and conversion work to grow qualified leads after launch.

underHood.title: Why we estimate websites this way
underHood.sub: Behind every visible design are page structure, SEO metadata, performance, CMS modeling, responsive layouts, forms, analytics, integrations, and launch prep. The calculator separates those layers.

seo.title: SEO structure
seo.body: Pages, metadata, headings, internal linking, and scalable landing architecture.

cms.title: CMS architecture
cms.body: CMS setup for editing pages, posts, cases, services, products, and multilingual content.

conversion.title: Conversion logic
conversion.body: Forms, CTAs, tracking, integrations, and UX decisions that turn visits into leads.

social.line: 50+ companies ran this calculator and launched with us. Some of them:
social.testimonialQuote: Before the new site we got 3 enquiries a month. Now we get 24.

items.q: What if my budget is below the calculator's lowest price?
items.a: If you have £800, we'll tell you straight that we can't deliver well at that price, and recommend someone we trust who can. We don't take projects we can't ship at the standard we want our name on.

items.q: What if the final price is higher than the calculator shows?
items.a: The calculator gives a realistic range 9 times out of 10. If something falls outside it, we tell you before signing, never after. The contract carries a fixed sum: no surprises, no scope-creep invoices.

items.q: Can I start with one package and upgrade later?
items.a: Yes. We design the architecture to scale. Launch as Starter; a year later, when traffic grows, add CMS, blog, or SEO, without rewriting from zero.

items.q: What if I need only one page?
items.a: We do landings starting at £1,500. Anything below that is either a templated solution or we're the wrong fit, and we'll point you to who isn't.

items.q: Do I need to know exactly what I want?
items.a: No. On the call we ask 10–15 questions and write the spec for you. Most clients arrive with a rough idea. That's normal.

items.q: What if I have my own designer or brand book?
items.a: Then −10–15% off the price and a shorter timeline. We work directly from your Figma or design files.

items.q: Can I see the code before paying full?
items.a: Yes. After the design phase we hand over the GitHub repo. You review, leave comments, then we continue. Transparent from day one, no code held hostage.

items.q: What's NOT included in the price?
items.a: Content copywriting, post-launch SEO promotion, and translation work. We can recommend trusted specialists for each, or quote them as add-ons. Everything else (design, code, CMS, hosting setup, 1-year warranty) is in the price.

getFinal.title: Send the brief — get a final price
getFinal.sub: We'll review your calculator result and send a project plan, timeline, and confirmed price range. Custom-coded websites only. If you need a quick £300 template, we're not the right fit.

controls.presetNote: Not sure what you need? Choose a preset and adjust it below.
controls.customizeNote: Use the recommended package as a starting point, or adjust the scope manually below.
controls.ecommercePagesHelp: Product pages are handled by store structure. This counts static pages like Home, About, Contact, Delivery, Terms.
controls.pagesHelp: Not sure how many pages you need? Usually a business website starts with Home, Services, About, Cases, Blog, Contact.
controls.sectionsHelp: Typical landing page includes 6-8 sections: hero, benefits, services, process, cases, FAQ, and CTA.
controls.pagesIncludedTpl: Included pages: {included}. Each extra page adds {extra}.
controls.sectionsIncludedTpl: Included sections: {included}. Each extra section adds {extra}.
controls.langNoteSeo: Each language requires its own SEO structure, content, and CMS setup.
controls.langNoteTranslations: Translations themselves can be handled separately if the client provides content.
controls.timelineHint: Faster launches require parallel delivery, extra coordination, and priority production slots.

summary.title: Estimate summary
summary.whatYouGet: What you get at this price
whyChangesItems: More pages = more design and development work
More languages = separate SEO and CMS structure
Advanced design = custom UI and interactions
summary.roiBody: At a 3% conversion rate and a £1,000 average order, the site pays for itself in 12–15 enquiries. Roughly 3–6 months of work. After that, it earns for years.
summary.scarcity: Next available start: in 2 weeks. 2 slots left this quarter.
summary.disclaimer: This is an estimate, not an invoice. Final price depends on exact requirements, integrations, design references, and content volume.

leadForm.title: Project brief
leadForm.sub: We'll review your calculator result and reply with a confirmed range, timeline, and next steps.
leadForm.name: Name
leadForm.briefPlaceholder: Tell us goals, timeline, and required integrations
leadForm.trustLine: No spam. You will receive a structured project response, not a generic sales script.
leadForm.success: Thanks! We received your brief and will reply with a confirmed range and next steps.
leadForm.after4hBody: You get a reply with a confirmed price and clarifying questions.
leadForm.after2dBody: 30-minute call: we walk through the project and show relevant cases.
leadForm.after1wBody: Final proposal — fixed price, fixed deadline, penalty clause for delays.
leadForm.testimonialQuote: Before the new site we got 3 enquiries a month. Now we get 24.
